name: Analyser
summary: Put it anywhere in a **Chain** element and extract frequency or time domain data for visualization.

props.name: bands
props.description: The number of data points (the `frequencyBinCount`)

props.name: smoothing
props.description: An alias for the [`smoothingTimeConstant`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/AnalyserNode/smoothingTimeConstant). Makes transitions between data points smoother.

props.name: bypass
props.description: For **Analyser**, bypassing will mean data will be zeroed.

props.name: mix
props.description: How much wet, versus dry, signal should come through. In most cases, this is a proportion of `1`. If `mix` is set to `0.75`, 75% of the signal will be wet and 25% dry.

methods.name: getTimeData
methods.description: Get a snapshot of the [time domain data](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/AnalyserNode/getByteTimeDomainData)

name: Audio
summary: The top-level **HYPERBLAM** element, setting the audio context for all element operations within it.

props.name: gain
props.description: The output gain, sent to the destination. Think of it as the master volume.

props.name: bpm
props.description: The tempo, in beats per minute. Is overruled by a **Sequencer** element, where present.

events.name: blamsources
events.description: Fired when all descendant sound sources are ready for playing.

name: Bands
summary: Draws frequency bands as rectangles, similar to a classic graphic equalizer.

props.name: count
props.description: The number of elements to render, mapping to data bands. If there are more elements than bands (taken from **Analyser** `bands`) then the pattern repeats.

props.name: shuffle
props.description: Whether to randomly redistribute data band references among the Shadow DOM-rendered elements.

props.name: analyser
props.description: A CSS selector for the **Analyser** element.

props.name: transport
props.description: A CSS selector representing the element used to start and stop listening to analyser data. This element must fire `blamstart` and `blamstop` events. At the time of writing, **Media**, **Pads**, and **Notes** do this.

props.name: fps
props.description: Frames per second. The frequency at which **Analyser** data is read.

props.name: time
props.description: Whether to take data from the time (rather than frequency) domain.

props.name: first
props.description: The first of the data bands to use. If `undefined`, it’s the very first band provided by the **Analyser**.

props.name: last
props.description: The last of the data bands to use. If `undefined`, it’s the very last band provided by the **Analyser**.

name: Bank
summary: Organizes samples into a single bank for provisioning a sample-based instrument like Pads or Notes.

props.name: event
props.description: By default, the **Bank** fetches and converts constituent samples on the `blamready` event, which is fired on the window when all elements have been defined.

props.name: from
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements from which events should be listened to. Defaults to `window` for **Bank**.

props.name: to
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements to affect in response to the event. Defaults to the **Bank** element itself, in this case, and should not be altered.

props.name: prop
props.description: Not applicable for **Bank**.

props.name: value
props.description: Not applicable for **Bank**.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

events.name: blambank
events.description: Fired when all the **Bank**’s samples have been converted to buffers and are ready for use. The data contains the buffers as `sounds`.

name: Bar
summary: Defines a single bar’s pattern, as handled by a parent **Track** element.

props.name: s
props.description: The space-separated pattern representing the distribution of notes or sounds. `0` represents a pause (skipped sound/beat).

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that any one sound in the `s` pattern will be played. Overrides a parent **Track’s** `chance`.

props.name: fill
props.description: Whether to play a fill (a randomized rearrangement of the bar’s pattern).

events.name: blam
events.description: Fired whenever one bar (defined by a child Bar element) ends and another begins. Useful for compositional changes, made on the fly. Instigated by an ancestor **Track** element.

name: Blam
summary: Handles events to affect a prop or `AudioParam`, or execute a method. Which of these three are applicable is automatically determined from the `prop` name supplied.

props.name: ramp
props.description: In beats. Where the `prop` is an `audioParam`, whether to ramp the value. For example, for `gain`, whether to fade/slide between the start and end values.

props.name: revert
props.description: When `revert` is applied, values do not _stick_. Once set, the next event reverts them to the original value (the one taken from the element during initialization). Used with a low `chance`, you create the effect of a regular value that just occasionally, and momentarily, strays.

props.name: from
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements from which events will be listened to. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultFrom` function’s return value.

props.name: to
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements to affect in response to the event. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultTo` function’s return value.

props.name: value
props.description: **Handle**-derived elements support special `value` syntax. Separate with `|` and one value is chosen at random. Separate with `~` and a value is chosen within the range `[x]~[n]`. Separate with ` ` (a single space) if you are supplying multiple args to an element’s method.

props.name: event
props.description: The event type to listen for. For many Event-based elements, this is the generic custom event `blam`.

props.name: prop
props.description: The property (which may be a method or map to an `audioParam`) to affect at the `event`.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

name: Chain
summary: For connecting together different effects, like in a pedalboard.

props.name: out
props.description: The `id` of the next element to connect to, in the signal chain. If not set, it connects to the ancestor **Audio** element.

props.name: mix
props.description: How much wet, versus dry, signal should come through. In most cases, this is a proportion of `1`. If `mix` is set to `0.75`, 75% of the signal will be wet and 25% dry.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to send _just_ the dry signal. If set, the `dry` signal is sent using the `wet` signal’s gain value, for parity.

name: Dial
summary: Add a child input of type `number` or `range` to control a target element’s prop, `audioParam`, or method.

props.name: event
props.description: Do not supply an `event` prop for this element. It must use the child input’s `input` event.

props.name: ramp
props.description: In beats. Where the `prop` is an `audioParam`, whether to ramp the value. For example, for `gain`, whether to fade/slide between the start and end values.

props.name: revert
props.description: When `revert` is applied, values do not _stick_. Once set, the next event reverts them to the original value (the one taken from the element during initialization). Used with a low `chance`, you create the effect of a regular value that just occasionally, and momentarily, strays.

props.name: from
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements from which events will be listened to. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultFrom` function’s return value.

props.name: to
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements to affect in response to the event. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultTo` function’s return value.

props.name: value
props.description: **Handle**-derived elements support special `value` syntax. Separate with `|` and one value is chosen at random. Separate with `~` and a value is chosen within the range `[x]~[n]`. Separate with ` ` (a single space) if you are supplying multiple args to an element’s method.

props.name: prop
props.description: The property (which may be a method or map to an `audioParam`) to affect at the `event`.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

name: Display
summary: Watches and renders the changing value of any property/attribute of any element.

props.name: element
props.description: A CSS selector representing the element to watch. Falls back to the `parentNode` where not supplied.

props.name: prop
props.description: The attribute defining the value to be displayed. If not supplied, nothing is displayed.

name: Echo
summary: An echo effect, using a delay node. Includes a lowpass filter for tape echo-like degradation.

props.name: beats
props.description: The interval of echos, relative to the bpm set by an ancestor **Audio** or **Sequencer** element.

props.name: feedback
props.description: The intensity of the effect. When applying a `cutoff`, be aware that high values can cause self-oscillation (and things can get very loud). For ear safety, the value is capped at `0.75`.

props.name: cutoff
props.description: A lowpass filter, applied inside the feedback loop, meaning echos get progressively _duller_ over time. Emulates the behavior of tape echo.

props.name: mix
props.description: Echo is a special case. The dry signal is always `1` and the wet signal is added on top.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to send _just_ the dry signal. If set, the `dry` signal is sent using the `wet` signal’s gain value, for parity.

name: Envelope
summary: An auxiliary envelope. Control any `audioParam` of any element by describing a curve for its value. Uses the closest **Sampler**-based element’s `blam` event.

props.name: curve
props.description: Comma separated pairs of numbers representing the envelope. Each pair defines the value and the time. By default, the time is a proportion of the sample’s duration. For example, `0.5` means half the sample’s duration.

props.name: beats
props.description: Time (the second value of each `curve` pair) is set in beats (relative to the inherited `bpm`) instead of as a proportion of the sample’s duration. In other words, use absolute values. Either way, all values are capped to the sample’s duration.

props.name: value
props.description: Not applicable. Use `curve` in its place.

props.name: prop
props.description: The `audioParam` to affect. Generic props are not applicable.

props.name: ramp
props.description: Not applicable. The curve is defined using `curve`.

props.name: from
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements from which events will be listened to. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultFrom` function’s return value.

props.name: to
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements to affect in response to the event. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultTo` function’s return value.

props.name: event
props.description: The event type to listen for. For many Event-based elements, this is the generic custom event `blam`.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

name: Filter
summary: A single-band filter effect. Important for coloring sound.

props.name: freq
props.description: The frequency for the filter, in hertz. The role of this frequency depends on the `type`.

props.name: q
props.description: The quality factor (often relating to resonance).

props.name: type
props.description: The type of filter. This can only be one of ‘lowpass’, ‘highpass’, ‘bandpass’, or ‘notch’.

props.name: mix
props.description: How much wet, versus dry, signal should come through. In most cases, this is a proportion of `1`. If `mix` is set to `0.75`, 75% of the signal will be wet and 25% dry.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to send _just_ the dry signal. If set, the `dry` signal is sent using the `wet` signal’s gain value, for parity.

name: Gain
summary: A basic gain (volume) effect. Can be automated with an LFO to create tremolo effects or an Envelope to affect attack or delay.

props.name: gain
props.description: The gain of the gain node. This is relative to the input gain. The default value, `1`, means _no change_.

props.name: mix
props.description: How much wet, versus dry, signal should come through. In most cases, this is a proportion of `1`. If `mix` is set to `0.75`, 75% of the signal will be wet and 25% dry.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to send _just_ the dry signal. If set, the `dry` signal is sent using the `wet` signal’s gain value, for parity.

name: Intersect
summary: Emits events with intersection data.

props.name: root
props.description: CSS selector representing the scollable area containing the observed elements. Defaults to the viewport.

props.name: steps
props.description: The granularity of intersection being observed. Where set to `10`, 10 events are fired, each with a ratio between `0.1` and `1`.

props.name: margin
props.description: Alias for the standard `rootMargin` supplied to the observer settings. CSS value that must be a `%` or `px` value.

events.name: blam
events.description: Emitted whenever an intersection occurs, carrying the intersection ratio as `x`, `on` (is intersection happening?), and `switch` (whether the intersection status changed).

name: Kbd
summary: Use a single keyboard key to trigger sounds, toggle props, or do just about anything.

props.name: key
props.description: The relevant key (corresponding to [`event.key`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/UI_Events/Keyboard_event_key_values))

props.name: ramp
props.description: In beats. Where the `prop` is an `audioParam`, whether to ramp the value. For example, for `gain`, whether to fade/slide between the start and end values.

props.name: revert
props.description: When `revert` is applied, values do not _stick_. Once set, the next event reverts them to the original value (the one taken from the element during initialization). Used with a low `chance`, you create the effect of a regular value that just occasionally, and momentarily, strays.

props.name: from
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements from which events will be listened to. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultFrom` function’s return value.

props.name: to
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements to affect in response to the event. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultTo` function’s return value.

props.name: value
props.description: **Handle**-derived elements support special `value` syntax. Separate with `|` and one value is chosen at random. Separate with `~` and a value is chosen within the range `[x]~[n]`. Separate with ` ` (a single space) if you are supplying multiple args to an element’s method.

props.name: event
props.description: The event type to listen for. For many Event-based elements, this is the generic custom event `blam`.

props.name: prop
props.description: The property (which may be a method or map to an `audioParam`) to affect at the `event`.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

name: Lfo
summary: A multi-purpose Low Frequency Oscillator (LFO). Modulate any `audioParam` of any element, including any other LFO.

props.name: prop
props.description: The `audioParam` to which the **LFO** connects. This may be mapped to a `prop` or exposed separately. Its value at the time of connection represents the _center_ value for oscillation.

props.name: type
props.description: The waveform for the oscillator. Can be ‘sine’, ‘square’, ‘sawtooth’, or ‘triangle’.

props.name: gain
props.description: For an LFO, the gain represents how much the `param` value changes in either direction. If the `param` value is `0.5` and the LFO `gain` value is `0.25`, the `param` value will oscillate between `0.25` and `0.75`, passing through `0.5` in both directions.

props.name: beats
props.description: The frequency of the LFO in beats, mapped to the `bpm` of an ancestor **Audio** or **Sequencer** element.

props.name: detune
props.description: The _relative_ frequency, in cents (`100` cents is 1 semitone). The default, `0`, means the frequency in `beats` is unchanged.

props.name: invert
props.description: Reverses the LFO polarity. `1` values become `-1` and vice versa.

name: Limiter
summary: A hard-wired limiter module that does its best to eliminate clipping. Best used at the end of a **Chain**.

props.name: mix
props.description: How much wet, versus dry, signal should come through. In most cases, this is a proportion of `1`. If `mix` is set to `0.75`, 75% of the signal will be wet and 25% dry.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to send _just_ the dry signal. If set, the `dry` signal is sent using the `wet` signal’s gain value, for parity.

name: Log
summary: Logs event data. Not to be confused with **Display** which tracks prop/param changes.

props.name: cumulative
props.description: Whether to log additively or to just log the last event’s data. If set, each successively rendered `<span>` will have `role="listitem"` and the element itself will take `role="list"`.

props.name: prop
props.description: The property belonging to the event’s data (the `CustomEvent` `detail` property). If not set (`null`), the whole of `event.detail` is stringified.

props.name: value
props.description: Not applicable for **Log**.

props.name: to
props.description: Not applicable for **Log**.

props.name: from
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements from which events will be listened to. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultFrom` function’s return value.

props.name: event
props.description: The event type to listen for. For many Event-based elements, this is the generic custom event `blam`.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

name: Media
summary: Makes an `<audio>` or `<video>` element an audio source and the beginning of a signal chain.

props.name: element
props.description: A CSS selector for an `<audio>` or `<video>` element. If not set, the element looks for an applicable descendant element.

props.name: out
props.description: The `id` of the next element in the chain. If not set, it defaults to an ancestor `<audio>` or `<video>` element.

events.name: blamsource
events.description: Fired when the audio can be played through (proxying the `canplaythrough` event).

name: Midi
summary: Connects with MIDI devices and converts MIDI events.

events.name: midi
events.description: A generic MIDI event, with data containing the MIDI `device` and `message`.

events.name: midiOn
events.description: Like the generic “midi” event but specific to MIDI _on_ messages.

name: Mod
summary: Extracts discrete values from LFOs to be used as multipliers. Great for automating regular prop values over time.

props.name: wave
props.description: The waveform for the built-in LFO. Can be ‘sine’, ‘square’, ‘sawtooth’, or ‘triangle’.

props.name: gain
props.description: Represents how much the multiplier value changes in either direction.

props.name: beats
props.description: The frequency of the built-in LFO in beats, mapped to the `bpm` of an ancestor **Audio** or **Sequencer** element.

props.name: invert
props.description: Reverses the LFO polarity. `1` values become `-1` and vice versa.

props.name: from
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements from which events will be listened to. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultFrom` function’s return value.

props.name: to
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements to affect in response to the event. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultTo` function’s return value.

props.name: value
props.description: **Handle**-derived elements support special `value` syntax. Separate with `|` and one value is chosen at random. Separate with `~` and a value is chosen within the range `[x]~[n]`. Separate with ` ` (a single space) if you are supplying multiple args to an element’s method.

props.name: event
props.description: The event type to listen for. For many Event-based elements, this is the generic custom event `blam`.

props.name: prop
props.description: The property (which may be a method or map to an `audioParam`) to affect at the `event`.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

name: Notes
summary: A pitched instrument, using multiple, randomized samples to create tonal variety.

props.name: root
props.description: The root note for the buffers/sounds where not supplied by the individual **Sample** element. Used to calculate pitch shifts.

props.name: notes
props.description: A set of notes from which to select at random.

props.name: bank
props.description: By default, the sample bank for the element will be a descendant Bank element. Or you can supply an `id` for a remote Bank element.

props.name: out
props.description: An `id` for the next element in the signal chain. If not supplied this defaults to a descendant **Chain** element or (if no **Chain** is included) the ancestor **Audio** element.

props.name: gain (`AudioParam`)
props.description: The gain level for samples played by this element. All subsequent gain settings in the signal chain are relative to this.

props.name: detune (`AudioParam`)
props.description: Maps to the buffer’s `detune` parameter. Set the detune value in cents. `100` means a half step (semitone) up and `-100` means a half step (semitone) down.

props.name: length
props.description: Foreshorten the sample. The value, in beats, represents the new length. Can be randomized using a **Blam** element for interesting effects.

props.name: start
props.description: Where, in beats, to start the sample playback. Maps to the `start` method’s `offset` prop. Where `reverse` is applied, the position is moved to the opposite end of the sample.

props.name: mute
props.description: Sets the `gain` to zero. To avoid _popping_ sounds, it slopes the `gain` using the `choke` value, if set, or `0.01` seconds.

props.name: choke
props.description: Whether to stop the previous sound when a new sound starts. Important for emulating instruments like bass. If set as a Boolean attribute, the gain of the previous sample is rapidly sloped to `0` over `0.01` seconds. Set an explicit value to adjust this.

props.name: reverse
props.description: Whether to play the reversed version of the sample. Set as a Boolean, there's a 100% chance. Set with a number/float sets the probability. For example, `reverse="0.25"` means a a 25% chance the sample will be played in reverse. It can only do so where the `<sample-blam>` element has `reverse` set (meaning the reversed sample was created).

props.name: robin
props.description: By default, samples are played at random and the same sample is never played twice in a row. With `robin` set, samples are cycled through in order.

methods.name: play
methods.description: Plays one of the associated Bank’s sounds at a given pitch.
methods.args: **cipher**: The note (such as ‘C4’) or `id` Where `undefined`, either a random note or the next note from `notes` is played, depending on whether `robin` is true, **time**: When, relative to the context’s `currentTime`, to play the sound. Immediately, if not set.

events.name: sounds
events.description: Fired when all of the associated Bank element’s buffers are ready for playing.

events.name: blam
events.description: Fired whenever a sound (buffer) is played.

name: On
summary: Handles `midiOn` events, typically for playing an instrument’s sounds.

props.name: device
props.description: The named MIDI device to listen to. If not set, the element listens to all connected devices.

props.name: root
props.description: The key on the MIDI keyboard from which all sounds will be mapped.

props.name: range
props.description: How many keys, from the `root`, will be mapped (will actively make sounds). If not set, it’s the whole keyboard.

props.name: spread
props.description: For non-tonal instruments like Pads. Whether to spread the individual sounds over the range of keys (one sound per key).

props.name: from
props.description: CSS selector. Must be a **Midi** element. If not set, looks for an ancestor **Midi** element.

props.name: to
props.description: CSS selector. If not set, it looks for a single **Sampler**-based element.

props.name: value
props.description: **Handle**-derived elements support special `value` syntax. Separate with `|` and one value is chosen at random. Separate with `~` and a value is chosen within the range `[x]~[n]`. Separate with ` ` (a single space) if you are supplying multiple args to an element’s method.

props.name: event
props.description: The event type to listen for. For many Event-based elements, this is the generic custom event `blam`.

props.name: prop
props.description: The property (which may be a method or map to an `audioParam`) to affect at the `event`.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

name: Pads
summary: An instrument optimized for drums and other rhythmic or non-melodic patterns.

props.name: bank
props.description: By default, the sample bank for the element will be a descendant Bank element. Or you can supply an `id` for a remote Bank element.

props.name: out
props.description: An `id` for the next element in the signal chain. If not supplied this defaults to a descendant **Chain** element or (if no **Chain** is included) the ancestor **Audio** element.

props.name: gain (`AudioParam`)
props.description: The gain level for samples played by this element. All subsequent gain settings in the signal chain are relative to this.

props.name: detune (`AudioParam`)
props.description: Maps to the buffer’s `detune` parameter. Set the detune value in cents. `100` means a half step (semitone) up and `-100` means a half step (semitone) down.

props.name: length
props.description: Foreshorten the sample. The value, in beats, represents the new length. Can be randomized using a **Blam** element for interesting effects.

props.name: start
props.description: Where, in beats, to start the sample playback. Maps to the `start` method’s `offset` prop. Where `reverse` is applied, the position is moved to the opposite end of the sample.

props.name: mute
props.description: Sets the `gain` to zero. To avoid _popping_ sounds, it slopes the `gain` using the `choke` value, if set, or `0.01` seconds.

props.name: choke
props.description: Whether to stop the previous sound when a new sound starts. Important for emulating instruments like bass. If set as a Boolean attribute, the gain of the previous sample is rapidly sloped to `0` over `0.01` seconds. Set an explicit value to adjust this.

props.name: reverse
props.description: Whether to play the reversed version of the sample. Set as a Boolean, there's a 100% chance. Set with a number/float sets the probability. For example, `reverse="0.25"` means a a 25% chance the sample will be played in reverse. It can only do so where the `<sample-blam>` element has `reverse` set (meaning the reversed sample was created).

props.name: robin
props.description: By default, samples are played at random and the same sample is never played twice in a row. With `robin` set, samples are cycled through in order.

events.name: sounds
events.description: Fired when all of the associated Bank element’s buffers are ready for playing.

events.name: blam
events.description: Fired whenever a sound (buffer) is played.

methods.name: play
methods.description: Plays one of the associated Bank’s sounds.
methods.args: **cipher**: The number (not zero-based) or `id` of the specific sound. Where `undefined`, either a random sound or the next sound is played, depending on whether `robin` is true, **time**: When, relative to the context’s `currentTime`, to play the sound. Immediately, if not set.

name: Pan
summary: Stereo panning for your signal chain. Gives instruments their space in the stereo field.

props.name: pan
props.description: The amount of panning. From `-1` (hard left) to `1` (hard right).

props.name: mix
props.description: How much wet, versus dry, signal should come through. In most cases, this is a proportion of `1`. If `mix` is set to `0.75`, 75% of the signal will be wet and 25% dry.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to send _just_ the dry signal. If set, the `dry` signal is sent using the `wet` signal’s gain value, for parity.

name: Phaser
summary: A classic phaser effect. The sound of a signal going in and out of phase with itself.

props.name: beats
props.description: The length of the phasing effect, relative to the `bpm`.

props.name: center
props.description: The frequency phasing centers around.

props.name: depth
props.description: The distance from the `center` frequency in both directions.

props.name: q
props.description: The quality factor. The resonance of the effect.

props.name: mix
props.description: How much wet, versus dry, signal should come through. In most cases, this is a proportion of `1`. If `mix` is set to `0.75`, 75% of the signal will be wet and 25% dry.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to send _just_ the dry signal. If set, the `dry` signal is sent using the `wet` signal’s gain value, for parity.

name: Props
summary: Converts **Analyser** data into CSS custom properties and (optionally) maps those values to selected elements. For each data band, places a custom property of the format `--[name]-[type]-[index]` on the root. In addition, `--[name]-[type]` represents the average value. For frequency data, this is effectively the signal strength. Finally, `--[name]-[type]-interval` represents the duration of one frame (based on the `fps` value). This is useful for CSS transitions.

props.name: selector
props.description: A CSS selector representing all the elements to which data values should be mapped. Each element’s `--[name]-[type]` property is mapped to a specific band (`--[name]-[type]: --[name]-[type]-[index]`), meaning all selected elements can be affected with a single declaration.

props.name: shuffle
props.description: Whether to randomly distribute data bands among target elements chosen with `selector`.

props.name: analyser
props.description: A CSS selector for the **Analyser** element.

props.name: transport
props.description: A CSS selector representing the element used to start and stop listening to analyser data. This element must fire `blamstart` and `blamstop` events. At the time of writing, **Media**, **Pads**, and **Notes** do this.

props.name: fps
props.description: Frames per second. The frequency at which **Analyser** data is read.

props.name: time
props.description: Whether to take data from the time (rather than frequency) domain.

props.name: first
props.description: The first of the data bands to use. If `undefined`, it’s the very first band provided by the **Analyser**.

props.name: last
props.description: The last of the data bands to use. If `undefined`, it’s the very last band provided by the **Analyser**.

name: Radios
summary: Takes a fieldset of radios to offer a multiple choice of target prop/param values.

props.name: event
props.description: Do not supply an `event` prop for this element. It must use a child fieldset’s `change` event.

props.name: value
props.description: Not applicable. The value is taken and parsed from the checked radio button.

props.name: from
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements from which events will be listened to. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultFrom` function’s return value.

props.name: to
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements to affect in response to the event. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultTo` function’s return value.

props.name: prop
props.description: The property (which may be a method or map to an `audioParam`) to affect at the `event`.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

name: Relay
summary: Takes one event and relays it to multiple (child) handler elements. Good for automating multiple changes at once.

props.name: mode
props.description: One of `'one'`, `'some'`, or `'all'`. Each time an event is received, which of the subject handler elements to activate. Importantly, elements’ `off` state is circumvented. Elements with `off` are _only_ activated by the **Relay**. Elements without `off` are activated independently _and_ via **Relay**.

props.name: prop
props.description: Not applicable for **Relay**. The `prop` is defined on each subject handler.

props.name: value
props.description: Not applicable for **Relay**. The `value` is defined on each subject handler.

props.name: from
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements from which events will be listened to. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultFrom` function’s return value.

props.name: to
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements to affect in response to the event. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultTo` function’s return value.

props.name: event
props.description: The event type to listen for. For many Event-based elements, this is the generic custom event `blam`.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

name: Repeat
summary: Group some **Track** element’s **Bars** and repeat the enclosed pattern/part `x` number of times.

props.name: x
props.description: How many times the enclosed pattern/part should be repeated. If `x="3"`, the **Bar** or **Bars** will play 3 times before the next **Bar** or **Repeat** element is played.

name: Reverb
summary: Convolution based reverb effect using a sample from a **Sample** element as the impulse response (IR).

props.name: src
props.description: The Sample element supplying the impulse response for convolution. Supply an `id` with this prop, or just supply a child Sample element.

props.name: cutoff
props.description: The cutoff frequency for the reverb. The lower the value, the _darker_ the reverb.

props.name: mix
props.description: How much wet, versus dry, signal should come through. In most cases, this is a proportion of `1`. If `mix` is set to `0.75`, 75% of the signal will be wet and 25% dry.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to send _just_ the dry signal. If set, the `dry` signal is sent using the `wet` signal’s gain value, for parity.

name: Rings
summary: Draws frequency bands as concentric rings or filled circles. Try not to get hypnotized.

props.name: fill
props.description: Whether to fill the SVG `<circle/>` elements. (The blend mode is set to `difference`, so superimposed circles are not obscured as such.)

props.name: count
props.description: The number of elements to render, mapping to data bands. If there are more elements than bands (taken from **Analyser** `bands`) then the pattern repeats.

props.name: shuffle
props.description: Whether to randomly redistribute data band references among the Shadow DOM-rendered elements.

props.name: analyser
props.description: A CSS selector for the **Analyser** element.

props.name: transport
props.description: A CSS selector representing the element used to start and stop listening to analyser data. This element must fire `blamstart` and `blamstop` events. At the time of writing, **Media**, **Pads**, and **Notes** do this.

props.name: fps
props.description: Frames per second. The frequency at which **Analyser** data is read.

props.name: time
props.description: Whether to take data from the time (rather than frequency) domain.

props.name: first
props.description: The first of the data bands to use. If `undefined`, it’s the very first band provided by the **Analyser**.

props.name: last
props.description: The last of the data bands to use. If `undefined`, it’s the very last band provided by the **Analyser**.

name: Sample
summary: Converts a sound file into a buffer that can be triggered as a sound by an instrument.

props.name: src
props.description: The path to the sample sound file. Typically, this would be an mp3.

props.name: root
props.description: The note the sample represents, if it is tonal. Used for calculating pitch changes.

props.name: reverse
props.description: Whether to save a reversed version of the sample as well. Used with instruments’ reverse property, which sets the probability of samples being played in reverse.

events.name: blamsource
events.description: Fired when the buffer has been created. The buffer itself is passed in the event data.

name: Saturator
summary: A saturation/distortion effect with multiple modes, attenuation through a dedicated `gain`, and two filters for cutting any unwanted low and high frequencies.

props.name: amount
props.description: The amount of saturation applied. Beware that very low numbers result in a drop in overall volume. Adjust the `wet` and `dry` signals to mix distortion with the incoming sound.

props.name: mode
props.description: The type of waveshaping algorithm applied. At the time of writing, 4 modes are offered. Check the source file for more. They formulas appear in the `makeCurve()` function.

props.name: lowcut
props.description: A highpass filter for removing any “booming” frequencies produced by applying the saturation.

props.name: highcut
props.description: A lowpass filter for taking out harsher frequencies from the high end.

props.name: gain
props.description: For attenuation purposes. Set very low by default, since the saturation adds a lot of its own gain. In testing, `0.1` seems to be just over [unity gain](https://www.sweetwater.com/insync/what-is-unity-gain-and-why-should-i-care/).

props.name: mix
props.description: How much wet, versus dry, signal should come through. In most cases, this is a proportion of `1`. If `mix` is set to `0.75`, 75% of the signal will be wet and 25% dry.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to send _just_ the dry signal. If set, the `dry` signal is sent using the `wet` signal’s gain value, for parity.

name: Sequencer
summary: Schedules future beats in a sequence and alerts subscribing elements.

props.name: bpm
props.description: The tempo in beats-per-minute. Overrules the ancestor Audio element’s `bpm` if set.

props.name: scale
props.description: The size of the interval in quarter-beats. `2` would mean each interval is, in effect, a half-beat.

props.name: swing
props.description: Applied as a Boolean attribute, it applies a swing of `0.333`, making adjacent beats the first and last beats of a triplet. Supply a number to adjust this value.

events.name: blam
events.description: Critically contains the time for the scheduled beat (relative to `currentTime`), but also supplies **Sequencer** and **Track** information.

methods.name: play
methods.description: Starts the sequencer, which begins emitting `blam` events at intervals according to the current `scale`.

methods.name: stop
methods.description: Ceases emitting `blam` events and resets all descendant **Track** elements, essentially rewinding each to the beginning of their first **Bar**’s `s` pattern.

methods.name: pause
methods.description: Ceases emitting `blam` events. Does not reset any constituent **Tracks**.

name: Switch
summary: Takes an input of type `checkbox` and uses it to change any target prop of any element.

props.name: event
props.description: Do not supply an `event` prop for this element. It must use the child input’s `input` event.

props.name: value
props.description: If a `value` is supplied, when the input in checked, the `value` is applied. When unchecked, it reapplies the `prop`’s initial value. If no `value` is applied the value is considered Boolean.

props.name: reverse
props.description: Whether to reverse the polarity of the checked state. Useful where the label and prop seem to contradict one another. For example, a switch labeled “filter” that turns a filter _on_ by making its bypass property `false`.

props.name: ramp
props.description: In beats. Where the `prop` is an `audioParam`, whether to ramp the value. For example, for `gain`, whether to fade/slide between the start and end values.

props.name: revert
props.description: When `revert` is applied, values do not _stick_. Once set, the next event reverts them to the original value (the one taken from the element during initialization). Used with a low `chance`, you create the effect of a regular value that just occasionally, and momentarily, strays.

props.name: from
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements from which events will be listened to. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultFrom` function’s return value.

props.name: to
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements to affect in response to the event. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultTo` function’s return value.

props.name: prop
props.description: The property (which may be a method or map to an `audioParam`) to affect at the `event`.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

name: Tap
summary: Takes a child button element and uses it to trigger any method, or to apply any prop or param value. If `aria-pressed` is supplied on page load, the button becomes a toggle and the value will switch between `true` and `false`.

props.name: event
props.description: The event type to listen for. For many Event-based elements, this is the generic custom event `blam`.

props.name: ramp
props.description: In beats. Where the `prop` is an `audioParam`, whether to ramp the value. For example, for `gain`, whether to fade/slide between the start and end values.

props.name: revert
props.description: When `revert` is applied, values do not _stick_. Once set, the next event reverts them to the original value (the one taken from the element during initialization). Used with a low `chance`, you create the effect of a regular value that just occasionally, and momentarily, strays.

props.name: from
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements from which events will be listened to. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultFrom` function’s return value.

props.name: to
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements to affect in response to the event. If not set, the node array defaults to the element’s `defaultTo` function’s return value.

props.name: value
props.description: **Handle**-derived elements support special `value` syntax. Separate with `|` and one value is chosen at random. Separate with `~` and a value is chosen within the range `[x]~[n]`. Separate with ` ` (a single space) if you are supplying multiple args to an element’s method.

props.name: prop
props.description: The property (which may be a method or map to an `audioParam`) to affect at the `event`.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

events.name: blam
events.description: Fired whenever the `<button>` is clicked. Where `to` is not set, `Tap` just fires this event. Then users can trigger handlers like `<relay-blam>` directly.

name: Track
summary: Corresponds to a **Sequencer** and uses child **Bar** elements to define repeating rhythmic patterns.

props.name: solo
props.description: When applied, all other **Track** elements belonging to the ancestor **Sequencer** are suspended (their sounds aren’t played).

props.name: overrides
props.description: A space-separated list of **Track** `id`s. **Tracks** (belonging to a common **Sequencer**) whose sounds will _not_ play when this **Track** plays a sound. Important for polymetric sequencing, to avoid “collisions”.

props.name: from
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements from which events should be listen to. Defaults to an ancestor **Sequencer** element for **Tracks**.

props.name: to
props.description: A CSS selector representing all elements that respond to stated events. Typically one or more **Sampler**-based elements.

props.name: suspend
props.description: Stops the **Track** from playing associated source sounds. Does not stop samples dead (like a mute). Instead, the final sound is allowed to play out.

props.name: value
props.description: **Handle**-derived elements support special `value` syntax. Separate with `|` and one value is chosen at random. Separate with `~` and a value is chosen within the range `[x]~[n]`. Separate with ` ` (a single space) if you are supplying multiple args to an element’s method.

props.name: event
props.description: The event type to listen for. For many Event-based elements, this is the generic custom event `blam`.

props.name: prop
props.description: The property (which may be a method or map to an `audioParam`) to affect at the `event`.

props.name: bypass
props.description: Whether to stop responding to events as they happen.

props.name: chance
props.description: The probability that the `event` will be responded to. From `0` to `1`.

events.name: blam
events.description: Fired whenever the current bar’s current step makes a sound. For a more regular series of events, use the parent **Sequencer’s** blam event instead.
